nameMeanings.name: Taye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Ethiopian: from the personal name Taye, which is interpreted as ‘he was seen’ in the Amharic language. — Note: Since Ethiopians do not have hereditary surnames, this name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
English (mainly Middlesex): variant of   Tay   1. This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Tay
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Soo, Chhay, Chin, Choo, Eng, Han, Heng, Khin, Li-Mei, Meng, Ming, Sang.  Vietnamese Dung, Loi, Thang, Thong, Tu.
English (Warwickshire, Worcestershire, and Staffordshire): variant of   Tee   1.
Jewish (from Poland): metonymic occupational name for a tea merchant, from central Yiddish tay ‘tea’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Thomas Taye was born about 1245, in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. She married Thomas Taye in 1265, in Rougham, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

ko.Rougham, Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Engla: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈
ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Thomas Taye(은)는 대략 1245년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1265년 영국 수폴크 루그함에서 Thomas Taye(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.
